issues: Headline is AI-invented; the OCR contains no headline for the Sockeyes article — it is a wire brief (CP) with no standalone headline.

Draft says Richmond 'trailed 1-0 after one period despite being outshot 39-23' — the OCR says they trailed 1-0 after the first period AND 2-1 after the second, and the outshot stat is straightforwardly reported, not framed as 'despite'.
Draft omits that the overtime period was specifically a 10-minute overtime period, a notable detail from the OCR.
Draft omits that the fourth game would be played in Richmond on Saturday.
Draft omits that Bill McDougall scored two goals and Curtis Chamberlin one for Humboldt.
Draft omits that Richmond took 13 of 25 minor penalties and the game's only major.
The date of the game was Wednesday (April 22, 1987) — this is correct in the draft, but the description should note it was a wire (CP) report.
The page also contains an unrelated NBA expansion franchise article (adjacent-column noise) which should be ignored.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Tony Bobbitt's goal 1:04 into a 10-minute overtime gave the Richmond Sockeyes a 4-3 win over the Humboldt Broncos, putting Richmond up 2-1 in the Centennial Cup Tier 2 western final series.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: In a CP wire report, Tony Bobbitt scored 1:04 into a 10-minute overtime period to give the Richmond Sockeyes a 4-3 victory over the Humboldt Broncos in Humboldt, Saskatchewan on April 22, 1987, staking Richmond to a 2-1 lead in the best-of-seven Tier 2 western final series. Dave Tomlinson, Mike Claringbull, and Rob Clarke scored the other Sockeyes goals; Bill McDougall (two) and Curtis Chamberlin replied for the Saskatchewan champion Broncos. Richmond was outshot 39-23, trailed 1-0 after one period and 2-1 after two, and took 13 of 25 minor penalties plus the game's only major. Game 4 was scheduled for Richmond on Saturday.
review.notes: The OCR contains an unrelated NBA expansion franchise article in an adjacent column — pure noise. The Sockeyes wire brief has no standalone headline; correctedHeadline has been left blank accordingly. Byline is a wire credit (CP), not a named reporter.
